dependencies.description: EVPN requires a BGP session with the L2VPN EVPN address family (AFI 25, SAFI 70) negotiated with a spine or route-reflector
dependencies.name: BGP session with L2VPN EVPN AFI/SAFI

dependencies.description: EVPN serves as the control plane for VXLAN; the NVE interface and VNI mappings must be configured for EVPN routes to be generated
dependencies.name: VXLAN overlay (NVE interface with VNIs)

dependencies.description: Route Distinguisher must be unique per VTEP; Route Targets must match (export on originator, import on receiver) for routes to be accepted
dependencies.name: Matching RD/RT configuration

dependencies.description: The underlay network must provide IP connectivity between VTEP loopback addresses for both BGP sessions and VXLAN data-plane encapsulation
dependencies.name: Underlay reachability between VTEPs

failure_modes.description: BGP EVPN session is established but no EVPN routes are being received from the peer. show bgp l2vpn evpn shows 0 routes from the remote VTEP or route-reflector.

resolutions.action: Verify address-family activation: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn summary' -- check session is up
Verify address-family activation: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn summary' -- check session is up
Verify address-family activation: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn summary' -- check session is up
Compare RT import/export on both VTEPs: 'show running-config | section evpn'
Compare RT import/export on both VTEPs: 'show running-config | section evpn'
Compare RT import/export on both VTEPs: 'show running-config | section evpn'
Check if route-map is applied to EVPN peer: 'show bgp neighbor <ip>' -- look for inbound/outbound policy
Check if route-map is applied to EVPN peer: 'show bgp neighbor <ip>' -- look for inbound/outbound policy
Check if route-map is applied to EVPN peer: 'show bgp neighbor <ip>' -- look for inbound/outbound policy
Verify RD is unique per VTEP and RT matches across VTEPs
Verify RD is unique per VTEP and RT matches across VTEPs
Verify RD is unique per VTEP and RT matches across VTEPs

root_causes.cause: L2VPN EVPN address family not activated under BGP neighbor
RT (route-target) mismatch between VTEPs
RD/RT auto-derivation failure
Route-map filtering EVPN routes

symptoms: show bgp l2vpn evpn shows 0 routes from peer
No remote MAC/IP entries learned via EVPN
show bgp l2vpn evpn summary shows 0 in PfxRcd column

suppress-ARP misconfiguration
EVPN instance not associated with the correct VNI

failure_modes.description: Inter-subnet routing fails across the VXLAN fabric. Hosts in different subnets (VLANs) cannot communicate through the overlay because EVPN type-5 IP prefix routes are missing or not being installed in the routing table.

Verify L3VNI configuration: 'show nve vni' -- look for L3 type VNI
Verify L3VNI configuration: 'show nve vni' -- look for L3 type VNI

Verify anycast gateway MAC is consistent: 'show fabric forwarding anycast-gateway-mac' (NX-OS)

root_causes.cause: L3VNI not configured
VRF-to-VNI mapping missing
Route-target import/export mismatch for L3VNI
Anycast gateway MAC mismatch between VTEPs

Hosts in different VLANs cannot communicate through overlay

failure_modes.description: The fabric detects duplicate MAC addresses -- the same MAC is being advertised by multiple VTEPs with increasing sequence numbers. The MAC may be frozen to prevent forwarding loops, causing connectivity loss for the host.

resolutions.action: Check for frozen MACs: 'show l2route evpn mac all' -- look for 'frozen' flag
Check for frozen MACs: 'show l2route evpn mac all' -- look for 'frozen' flag
Check for frozen MACs: 'show l2route evpn mac all' -- look for 'frozen' flag
Identify which VTEPs are advertising the MAC: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2' -- filter by MAC
Identify which VTEPs are advertising the MAC: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2' -- filter by MAC
Identify which VTEPs are advertising the MAC: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2' -- filter by MAC
If legitimate multi-homing, configure ESI on both VTEPs
If legitimate multi-homing, configure ESI on both VTEPs
If legitimate multi-homing, configure ESI on both VTEPs
If physical loop, trace cabling and shut offending port
If physical loop, trace cabling and shut offending port
If physical loop, trace cabling and shut offending port
Clear frozen MAC after fixing root cause: 'clear l2route evpn mac <mac>'
Clear frozen MAC after fixing root cause: 'clear l2route evpn mac <mac>'
Clear frozen MAC after fixing root cause: 'clear l2route evpn mac <mac>'

root_causes.cause: Physical loop in fabric (e.g., miscabled server NIC)
Host connected to multiple VTEPs without proper ESI multi-homing
VM migration without proper MAC mobility configuration
Misconfigured static MAC on multiple switches

symptoms: Syslog: 'duplicate MAC detected'
MAC address frozen in EVPN table
Host intermittently losing connectivity
Rapidly incrementing sequence numbers in type-2 routes

failure_modes.description: In a centralized gateway (CGW) design, the border leaf's BVI MAC is not being installed in the MAC address table on remote leaf switches. Traffic destined to the gateway IP is flooded or dropped because the gateway MAC is unknown.

resolutions.action: Verify BVI is up on CGW: 'show interface BVI <N>'
Verify BVI is up on CGW: 'show interface BVI <N>'
Verify BVI is up on CGW: 'show interface BVI <N>'
Check type-2 route for BVI MAC: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2' — filter by BVI MAC
Check type-2 route for BVI MAC: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2' — filter by BVI MAC
Check type-2 route for BVI MAC: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2' — filter by BVI MAC

Verify RT import on remote leaf matches RT export on CGW
Verify L2VNI is in the correct bridge-domain with the BVI
Verify L2VNI is in the correct bridge-domain with the BVI
Verify L2VNI is in the correct bridge-domain with the BVI

root_causes.cause: BVI MAC not being advertised via EVPN type-2 from CGW
RT import mismatch preventing BVI MAC route from being accepted on leaf
BVI interface down on the CGW border leaf
L2VNI not associated with the BVI's bridge-domain on CGW

failure_modes.description: When a link in an Ethernet Segment (ESI multi-homing) goes down, traffic forwarding to the affected host is delayed for 3-10 seconds while the EVPN control plane converges. The DF (Designated Forwarder) election and mass-withdrawal of type-1 routes take longer than expected.

resolutions.action: Enable type-1 per-ES (Ethernet Auto-Discovery per-ES) route for fast mass-withdrawal
Enable type-1 per-ES (Ethernet Auto-Discovery per-ES) route for fast mass-withdrawal
Enable type-1 per-ES (Ethernet Auto-Discovery per-ES) route for fast mass-withdrawal
Tune DF election timers if available on the platform
Tune DF election timers if available on the platform
Tune DF election timers if available on the platform
Consider BFD on the ESI link for faster failure detection
Consider BFD on the ESI link for faster failure detection
Consider BFD on the ESI link for faster failure detection
Verify type-1 route behavior: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi <ESI>'
Verify type-1 route behavior: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi <ESI>'
Verify type-1 route behavior: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi <ESI>'

root_causes.cause: Type-1 per-EVI withdraw/re-advertise takes BGP convergence time
Remote VTEPs must process type-1 withdrawal before updating forwarding
DF election timer adds delay before new DF starts forwarding
Large number of MACs behind ESI increases convergence time

failure_modes.scenario: ESI multi-homing convergence delay after link failure
symptoms: 3-10 second traffic loss after ESI member link failure
show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 shows withdrawal and re-advertisement
show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 shows DF election changes
Remote VTEPs take time to update forwarding to backup path

failure_modes.description: Legitimate IP address mobility (e.g., DHCP re-assignment or container orchestration) triggers EVPN duplicate IP detection. The IP address is frozen on the fabric, preventing the new host from communicating even though the MAC is different.

resolutions.action: Clear frozen IP: 'clear l2route evpn mac-ip frozen-ip <ip>' or 'clear l2route evpn mac-ip all'
Clear frozen IP: 'clear l2route evpn mac-ip frozen-ip <ip>' or 'clear l2route evpn mac-ip all'
Clear frozen IP: 'clear l2route evpn mac-ip frozen-ip <ip>' or 'clear l2route evpn mac-ip all'
Increase duplicate detection thresholds: 'evpn host-mobility duplicate-default-timer' or 'evpn host-mobility threshold'
Increase duplicate detection thresholds: 'evpn host-mobility duplicate-default-timer' or 'evpn host-mobility threshold'
Increase duplicate detection thresholds: 'evpn host-mobility duplicate-default-timer' or 'evpn host-mobility threshold'
If IP mobility is expected, consider increasing the max-moves count
If IP mobility is expected, consider increasing the max-moves count
If IP mobility is expected, consider increasing the max-moves count
Verify which MAC was last bound to the IP: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2' filter by IP
Verify which MAC was last bound to the IP: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2' filter by IP
Verify which MAC was last bound to the IP: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2' filter by IP

root_causes.cause: IP reuse faster than duplicate detection timer
DHCP server reassigning IP to new host while old entry still in EVPN
Container/VM orchestration moving IPs between MACs rapidly
Duplicate detection thresholds too aggressive for the environment

failure_modes.scenario: IP mobility duplicate detection causing host freeze
symptoms: Host with reassigned IP cannot communicate
Syslog: 'duplicate IP detected' or 'IP address frozen'
show l2route evpn mac-ip all shows frozen IP entry
Different MAC addresses advertising the same IP from different VTEPs

failure_modes.description: A physical Layer-2 loop on the access side (e.g., miscabled server NIC, rogue switch) triggers storm-control on the access interface (AC), which shuts down the port. Since the port is part of an EVPN EVI, the associated type-2 routes are withdrawn, causing all hosts behind that port to lose connectivity.

resolutions.action: Identify the loop source: check MAC flap logs before shutdown event
Identify the loop source: check MAC flap logs before shutdown event
Identify the loop source: check MAC flap logs before shutdown event
Fix physical cabling or remove rogue device
Fix physical cabling or remove rogue device
Fix physical cabling or remove rogue device
Recover port: 'shutdown' then 'no shutdown', or 'errdisable recovery cause storm-control'
Recover port: 'shutdown' then 'no shutdown', or 'errdisable recovery cause storm-control'
Recover port: 'shutdown' then 'no shutdown', or 'errdisable recovery cause storm-control'
Add BPDU guard and storm-control with 'shutdown' action on access ports
Add BPDU guard and storm-control with 'shutdown' action on access ports
Add BPDU guard and storm-control with 'shutdown' action on access ports

root_causes.cause: Physical L2 loop on access side (miscabled NIC, rogue device)
Broadcast storm exceeding storm-control threshold
Missing STP BPDU guard on access ports

failure_modes.scenario: L2 loop causing AC interface shutdown via storm-control
symptoms: Access port error-disabled or shutdown by storm-control
Multiple hosts behind the port lose connectivity simultaneously
Syslog: 'storm-control action shutdown' or 'err-disabled'
MAC flapping detected before shutdown
EVPN type-2 routes for affected hosts withdrawn

failure_modes.description: The 'system nve infra-vlans' cost-out feature or explicit 'nve cost-out' command has been applied (intentionally during maintenance or accidentally left on), causing the VTEP to withdraw all EVPN routes and stop attracting overlay traffic. The NVE interface remains up but the VTEP is effectively isolated from the fabric.

resolutions.action: Check for explicit cost-out: 'show running-config | include cost-out'
Check for explicit cost-out: 'show running-config | include cost-out'
Check for explicit cost-out: 'show running-config | include cost-out'
Remove cost-out: 'no nve cost-out' under NVE interface or 'no system nve cost-out'
Remove cost-out: 'no nve cost-out' under NVE interface or 'no system nve cost-out'
Remove cost-out: 'no nve cost-out' under NVE interface or 'no system nve cost-out'
Verify infra-VLANs are properly configured: 'show system nve infra-vlans'
Verify infra-VLANs are properly configured: 'show system nve infra-vlans'
Verify infra-VLANs are properly configured: 'show system nve infra-vlans'
Check vPC status if cost-out is automatic: 'show vpc'
Check vPC status if cost-out is automatic: 'show vpc'
Check vPC status if cost-out is automatic: 'show vpc'

root_causes.cause: Explicit 'nve cost-out' applied and not removed after maintenance
Missing infra-VLANs configuration causing automatic cost-out
vPC peer-link failure triggering cost-out on secondary

failure_modes.scenario: Misconfigured cost-out preventing VTEP from attracting traffic
symptoms: VTEP not receiving any overlay traffic despite NVE being up
show bgp l2vpn evpn shows 0 advertised routes
Remote VTEPs do not show this VTEP in 'show nve peers'
All hosts behind this VTEP unreachable from the fabric

messages.description: Advertises per-EVI and per-ES routes for fast convergence and split-horizon filtering in multi-homing scenarios
messages.name: Type-1 (Ethernet Auto-Discovery)
messages.when_sent: When ESI is configured for multi-homing

messages.description: Advertises host MAC address and optionally the associated IP address. This is the most common EVPN route type, used for MAC learning across the fabric.
messages.name: Type-2 (MAC/IP Advertisement)
messages.when_sent: When new MAC or MAC/IP is learned locally

messages.description: Builds the ingress replication list for BUM traffic handling. Each VTEP joining a VNI advertises a type-3 route so other VTEPs know where to replicate BUM frames.
messages.name: Type-3 (Inclusive Multicast Ethernet Tag)

messages.description: Used for Designated Forwarder (DF) election in multi-homing scenarios. VTEPs sharing an Ethernet Segment exchange type-4 routes to elect a DF for each VLAN.
messages.name: Type-4 (Ethernet Segment)

messages.description: Advertises IP prefix routes for inter-subnet routing across the VXLAN fabric. Enables symmetric IRB (Integrated Routing and Bridging) with L3VNI.
messages.name: Type-5 (IP Prefix)
messages.when_sent: When IP prefix is learned or redistributed into EVPN

purpose: Ethernet VPN -- BGP-based control plane for VXLAN providing MAC/IP advertisement, multi-homing, and integrated routing

states.description: BGP L2VPN EVPN session is not established. The underlying TCP/179 session to the BGP EVPN peer (spine, route-reflector) has not come up, so no EVPN routes can be exchanged.
states.name: BGP_SESSION_DOWN

states.description: BGP EVPN session is established with the L2VPN EVPN address family negotiated. EVPN routes can be exchanged. This is the steady state when routes are actively being advertised and received.
states.name: BGP_SESSION_UP

states.description: EVPN Instance (EVI) is operational. The VNI is up, BGP session is established, RD/RT are configured, and EVPN routes are being advertised for this instance.
states.name: EVI_ACTIVE

states.description: EVPN Instance is not operational. The underlying VNI may be down, BGP session lost, or RD/RT misconfigured, preventing route advertisement for this instance.
states.name: EVI_INACTIVE

timers.description: Time window to detect MAC mobility events. When a MAC address moves between VTEPs, the sequence number in the EVPN type-2 route is incremented. This timer controls the detection window.
timers.name: MAC mobility sequence timer
timers.on_expiry: MAC mobility sequence frozen; duplicate MAC detection may trigger
timers.tuning_advice: Lower values detect mobility faster but may cause false positives during VM migration storms. Higher values are more tolerant but slower to detect loops.

timers.description: Hold-down time when a duplicate MAC is detected. During this period the MAC address is frozen and not re-learned to prevent forwarding loops.
timers.name: Duplicate MAC detection hold-down
timers.tuning_advice: If legitimate VM migrations trigger duplicate detection, increase the max-moves threshold before adjusting this timer. The hold-down should be long enough to allow an operator to investigate.

transitions.trigger: BGP session established with L2VPN EVPN address family negotiated
BGP session lost (TCP failure, hold timer, notification)
VNI operational and BGP session up; RT/RD configured
VNI goes down or BGP session lost

notes.note: EVPN VXLAN supported on Catalyst 9000. Uses 'l2vpn evpn' global config and 'address-family l2vpn evpn' under BGP. Distributed anycast gateway supported.
EVPN is a core feature for L2VPN services. Configured under 'evpn' and 'l2vpn bridge group'. Supports EVPN-VPWS, EVPN-MPLS, and EVPN-VXLAN. Multi-homing with ESI LAG natively supported.

IOS-XR EVPN supports Ethernet Segment with active-active multi-homing natively. DF election uses 'Preference-based' algorithm by default (mod-based also available). 'show evpn ethernet-segment' shows ESI state, DF election results, and convergence timers.

EVPN requires 'nv overlay evpn'. Configure under 'router bgp' with 'address-family l2vpn evpn'. Supports distributed anycast gateway with 'fabric forwarding anycast-gateway-mac'. Type-5 routes require L3VNI with 'vrf context' and SVI for L3VNI.

EVPN duplicate MAC/IP detection default: 5 moves in 180 seconds triggers freeze for 30 seconds. In environments with frequent VM migrations or container orchestration, increase thresholds with 'evpn host-mobility duplicate-default-timer' and 'evpn host-mobility threshold'. Frozen entries must be cleared manually with 'clear l2route evpn mac' or 'clear l2route evpn mac-ip'.

In vPC EVPN deployments, 'advertise-pip' controls next-hop advertisement. Without it, all routes use the shared VIP (anycast VTEP IP). With 'advertise-pip', Type-5 (IP prefix) routes use the individual PIP as next-hop while Type-2 (MAC/IP) routes still use VIP. Without advertise-pip, inter-subnet traffic may unnecessarily traverse the peer-link.

'nve cost-out' or 'system nve cost-out' withdraws all EVPN routes from this VTEP, effectively removing it from the fabric without shutting down NVE. Used for graceful maintenance. CRITICAL: verify cost-out is removed after maintenance — it is a common cause of 'VTEP up but no traffic' issues.

EVPN Type-1 (Ethernet Auto-Discovery) per-ES route enables fast mass-withdrawal when an ESI member link fails. Without per-ES routes, each MAC behind the ESI must be individually withdrawn via type-2 updates, which is much slower for large MAC tables.

For vPC VXLAN EVPN fabrics, 'system nve infra-vlans <vlan-list>' designates VLANs carried over the peer-link for infrastructure purposes (backup routing, BUM traffic transfer). These VLANs must be present and trunked on the peer-link. Missing infra-VLANs can cause silent forwarding failures for BUM traffic and backup routing.
